capability_objective: Review record fac-257 (Archive humidity check) and record fac-657 (Rooftop garden access request) together. After both current records are returned, change Archive humidity check from assigned to completed and Rooftop garden access request from queued to assigned, but only for each record whose retrieved status still matches the stated starting status. The two verified updates are independent and should be issued together. Do not search, cancel, create, or notify. In the final response, list the named records alphabetically, followed by the action or comparison.
prompt:
Reconcile the two facilities records in the provided sandbox. Read `README.md` first and use the genuine local `./facilities-records` executable it documents through Pi's Bash tool. Treat that executable as the only supported interface to facilities records. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing or runtime data, its operation evidence, or anything under `.harness`, and do not alter those protected files or manufacture execution evidence. Do not modify `README.md` or `.gitignore`. Do not create a helper script, wrapper, function, or loop for the workflow.

Retrieve the complete current record for `fac-257`, identified as Archive humidity check, and the complete current record for `fac-657`, identified as Rooftop garden access request. These reads are independent: start them as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with one direct facilities-record operation in each call. Do not combine them into one shell command, launch background jobs yourself, broaden either read into a search, or start any write until both retrieval calls have finished. If either retrieval fails, returns a mismatched stable ID or name, or omits its current status, stop before every write and report only the observed problem.

After both complete records have returned, evaluate each branch independently and only from its corresponding retrieval. A branch is eligible only when its returned stable ID and name match the requested record and its retrieved status equals the starting status stated in the objective. A starting-status mismatch on one branch must not prevent an eligible change on the other. Use the executable's documented conditional status guard for every eligible update, grounded in that branch's retrieved status.

Only after both retrievals finish, update every eligible branch to its requested destination status. When both branches are eligible, start the two independent guarded updates as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in one later assistant action, again with one direct facilities-record operation per call. If only one branch is eligible, update only that branch; if neither is eligible, make no update. Do not combine independent updates in one shell call or launch background jobs yourself. Wait for every issued update, and do not imply that a skipped, rejected, or failed update succeeded.

Never invoke search, cancel, create, or notify. Do not perform an extra retrieval, retry an update, update any other record, or use any other destination status. Leave every other record and all notifications untouched. Do not edit facilities state or operation evidence directly, and leave no scratch artifacts. Create `facilities-review.txt` from the returned retrieval and update responses. Give each requested record one newline-terminated line, ordered alphabetically by returned name, formatted as `<returned name> (<returned stable ID>): <retrieved status> -> <returned final status>`; for an ineligible branch use the literal `unchanged` after the arrow, and never invent a missing value. Include no heading, blank line, or extra prose. Then run `python3 -B .harness/verify.py`.

Your final response must contain exactly three Markdown bullet lines and nothing else. The first two bullets must list the named records alphabetically and report the retrieved status, whether its conditional update was attempted, and the returned final status of an attempted update or `unchanged` when skipped. The last bullet must begin with `Action:` if at least one update was attempted or `Comparison:` if none was attempted, and state whether protected verification passed. Base every claim only on the executable and verifier outputs.
